feedback.text: The digital fairness act sounds really promising and I would like to also add the issue of the video game industry into discussion. The video game industry has been using predatory, and in certain instances even arguably illegal practices in order to mislead and take advantage of consumers. The industry as a whole has little regulation, and this impacts me and millions of other users directly. One of the best examples I can give is the fact that video game companies sell products in both physical and digital stores, while hiding in the terms and conditions of said products terms that directly take away ownership of products from paying customers. A lot of videogames are right now being sold as products, and when a consumer purchases a product, there is expectation of ownership. What some video game companies are doing is hiding behind terms and conditions that state that the consumer does not own the product and that the company can revoke access for ANY reason at ANY time, with little to no recourse from the customer. As I stated earlier, when purchasing a product, the consumer has , unless being told otherwise from the get-go, the expectation of owning the product. These practices undermine and mislead consumers, all the while being potentially in violation with EU regulations. Another practice these company engage in, is ending support for products ,which more often then not leaves consumers who paid for the product unable to access it. While ending support for products is expected at some point, these companies offer no options for customers, and as a consumer, it feels like the company is more or less stealing what is supposed to be your property. The effects of these practices are already being felt throughout consumers, as hundreds of products have been ''destroyed" by companies, leaving consumers mislead and angry. It has gotten to the point ,that a community of consumers has created an European citizens initiative called ''Stop Killing Games", which at the time of writing has amassed over 1.4 million signatures. The Digital Fairness Act can be a solution to a lot of the problems raised in the initiative, especially since said problems coincide with the goal of the Digital Fairness Act. Thank you for reading and I hope this comment from an EU citizen is taken into consideration .
